title: Add verification module test
Add reviewer prompt template test
Add engineer module test
Improve error messages in projects.ts
Add CLI test
Add work_detection test
Add isBotRunning catalogdna test
Add parseReviewerResponse test
Add atomic write safety test
Add tailProgressLog test
Skip reviewer on empty diff
Auto-update bot/work to HEAD
Add --json flag to status
Add digest writer tests
Add dispatcher picker tests
Add worktree detection tests
Add engineer error handling test
Add projects CLI command
Add log --lines test
Add type guards
Programmatic hands-off check
Skip verification on empty diff
Add init command
Validate project paths during load
Add history command
Add doctor command
Improve engineer spawn error messages
Add history --format=compact
Session summary in PROGRESS.jsonl
Init command edge case tests
Add clean command (implemented by interactive session)

Add usage examples to each CLI command's help output — e.g., session shows 'generalstaff session --budget=480  # overnight 8-hour run', cycle shows 'generalstaff cycle --project=myapp --dry-run'. Update the main --help too.
In dispatcher.ts, add a tiebreaker to scoreProjects: when two projects have the same score, prefer the one with fewer total_cycles (spread work more evenly). Add a test for this behavior.
In session.ts, if a cycle returns verification_failed, log a one-line summary to stderr with the reason so the user sees it even when output is scrolling. Add test.

Add a test that validates the full CLI help output includes all registered commands: session, cycle, status, stop, start, log, projects, init, history, doctor, clean. Ensures new commands don't get forgotten in help text.
Add a session.test.ts with tests for the writeDigest function: verify it creates the digest file, includes all cycle results, formats SHA ranges, and handles empty results array

In dispatcher.ts, add a function estimateSessionPlan(projects, fleet, budgetMinutes) that returns a preview of which projects would be picked and how many cycles each would get, without executing anything. Add test.
Add a formatDuration helper function to a new src/format.ts module that converts seconds to human-readable strings (45s, 2m30s, 1h15m). Use it in history and digest output. Add tests.
In work_detection.ts, add a function countRemainingWork(project) that returns the number of pending tasks (not just boolean hasMoreWork). Use it in the session summary output. Add test.
Add a projects.test.ts test that validates warnProjectPaths with a real git repo (use the GeneralStaff repo itself as the valid path) and a non-git directory

Wire estimateSessionPlan into the session command — before running cycles, print a preview table showing which projects will be picked and how many cycles each gets. Only show when not --dry-run. Add to session output.
Add a generalstaff task command to cli.ts with subcommands: 'task list --project=<id>' shows pending tasks, 'task add --project=<id> <title>' appends a new task to tasks.json. Include tests.
In the history command output, add ANSI color codes: green for verified, red for verification_failed, yellow for verified_weak, gray for cycle_skipped. Only colorize when stdout is a TTY.
Add a session.test.ts test that validates the empty-cycle guard exits after 3 consecutive empty diffs, and that capped projects get added to the skip set

In audit.ts, add a function summarizeCosts that counts reviewer_invoked events from PROGRESS.jsonl and estimates total token usage based on prompt_length. Wire into the history --format=compact output as an optional column.

Add a generalstaff summary command to cli.ts that prints a one-screen overview: total cycles across all projects, verified/failed/skipped counts, total duration, test count (run bun test --reporter=json to get count), and tasks remaining. Like a dashboard snapshot.

In projects.ts, add a validateHandsOff function that checks each hands_off pattern against the project's actual file tree (git ls-files) and warns about patterns that don't match anything — helps users catch typos in their hands-off config. Add test.
Add a test for extractChangedFiles in cycle.ts — verify it handles renames (diff --git a/old b/new), binary files, and files with spaces in their names
In state.ts, add a getProjectSummary function that aggregates fleet state + project state + remaining tasks into a single object. Use it in the status --json output for richer data. Add test.
Add a generalstaff version command (separate from --version flag) that shows version, bun version, platform, and projects.yaml location. Useful for bug reports.

In cycle.ts, add a diffSummaryStats function that parses the diff to count files changed, insertions, and deletions. Include these stats in the cycle_end PROGRESS entry and the digest output. Add test.

Add a generalstaff config command to cli.ts that pretty-prints the current projects.yaml config (parsed and validated) — shows each project with all resolved defaults. Useful for debugging config issues. Add test.

In work_detection.ts, add support for a third work_detection mode 'git_issues' that counts open issues on the project's git remote via 'git log --oneline origin/master..HEAD' as a proxy for pending work. Add test with mock.
Add a test for the session plan preview (estimateSessionPlan) with multiple projects at different priorities — verify the picker alternates correctly and respects the per-project cap
In format.ts, add a formatBytes helper (1024 -> 1.0 KB, 1048576 -> 1.0 MB) for displaying diff sizes and log file sizes in the history and summary commands. Add tests.

In src/clean.ts, add a log-rotation step: delete files in logs/ older than N days (N configurable via --log-days=<n> flag, default 30). Surface count of deleted files in the clean output. Add tests covering files on both sides of the threshold and a custom --log-days override.

Add a 'generalstaff digest' command to cli.ts that cats a digest file to stdout. Support --latest (most recent file in digests/) and --date=YYYYMMDD (first digest from that day). If no digests exist, print a clear message. Add tests.

In src/session.ts, improve live console output between cycles: after each cycle, print 'Cycle N completed: <project> — <outcome>' and the remaining budget in minutes (use formatDuration). Makes interactive observation of long sessions readable. Add a test that captures stderr/stdout and asserts the format.
In src/summary.ts, add a Disk Usage section to the fleet summary dashboard: total bytes in logs/, digests/, and state/. Use formatBytes for display. Handle missing directories (0 B). Add test.

Add a test pinning formatBytes behavior for fractional sub-KB inputs (e.g. 1023.5 bytes). Either keep current behavior (returns '1023.5 B') and add an explicit test documenting it, or change the implementation to floor sub-KB values and update existing tests accordingly. Include a comment in format.ts explaining the chosen convention.

Add a 'generalstaff digest --list' subcommand that enumerates all digest files in digests/ with their date, total cycles, and verified/failed/skipped breakdown (parsed from the digest markdown). If no digests exist, print a clear message. Sort newest first. Add tests covering empty dir, one digest, and multiple digests.

In the 'history' command, add --since=YYYYMMDD and --until=YYYYMMDD filter flags that filter cycles by started_at. Reject malformed date input with a clear error (not a silent empty result). Add tests covering valid ranges, out-of-range filters, and malformed input.

Add --format=json to 'generalstaff summary' that emits the FleetSummary object as JSON (same data as the text dashboard, machine-readable). Include tests verifying JSON parses and matches the text output's counts.

Add CLI subcommand 'generalstaff task done --project=<id> --task=<task-id>' to mark a single task as done without hand-editing tasks.json. Error if project or task not found. Print a notice (don't error) if already done. Add tests for success, not-found, already-done.

In tasks.ts loadTasks, surface JSON parse errors instead of silently returning an empty list (current behavior misleads users into thinking there's 'no work' when really their tasks.json is malformed). On parse failure, log a clear error with the file path and rethrow or return a typed failure. Update callers to handle the new failure mode gracefully. Add tests covering malformed JSON, empty file, and valid-but-not-array structure.

Add --max-cycles=N flag to 'generalstaff session' as an alternative stopping condition alongside --budget. If both specified, stop at whichever hits first. Log which limit triggered ('max-cycles' vs 'budget'). Add tests covering max-cycles-hits-first, budget-hits-first, and default (only --budget).

In audit.ts summarizeCosts, add per-project breakdown of reviewer_invoked events (currently aggregated across fleet). Return { byProject: Record<string, {invocations, estTokens}>, total: {...} }. Wire into 'history --costs' output as an extra column when --project is not specified. Add tests.

Add --json flag to 'generalstaff digest' (pair with --list/--latest/--date from gs-057). Emits the same data as the markdown digest but as a structured JSON object (duration, cycle count, per-cycle results array with outcome/sha/diff_stats). Add tests.

Add test coverage for countCommitsAhead in cycle.ts. Use a tmp git repo fixture: init repo, make a commit on master, create a branch, verify count=0. Add a commit on the branch, verify count=1. Merge branch into master, verify count=0. Also verify returns 0 when branch doesn't exist.

Add 'generalstaff bot-status [--project=<id>]' CLI command that prints the bot branch's commit count ahead of HEAD for each project (using countCommitsAhead). Indicates pending unmerged work. Format: 'myproject: 2 commit(s) on bot/work not yet on master'. Add tests.

In work_detection.ts, add a 'git_unmerged' mode that counts commits on bot/work ahead of master as a proxy for pending work (alternative to tasks_json for projects without an explicit task file). Add VALID_WORK_DETECTION entry, cycle.ts dispatch, and tests.
Add a formatPercent helper to src/format.ts (0.42 -> '42%', 1.0 -> '100%', 0 -> '0%', handles NaN/Infinity/negatives by returning '?'). Include tests covering boundaries and invalid inputs.

In src/summary.ts, add a per-project success-rate line to the Cycles section of the fleet summary: 'Success rate: NN% (X/Y verified)' using formatPercent. Only shown when a project has at least one cycle. Add test.

Add a 'generalstaff log --level=error' filter that shows only cycle_skipped, verification_failed, and entries with event names ending in _error from PROGRESS.jsonl. Pair with existing --lines/--project flags. Add tests.

Add test coverage for the OpenRouter reviewer code path in reviewer.ts. Mock the global fetch to return a well-formed chat-completion response; verify invokeOpenRouterReviewer builds the correct request body (model, messages, temperature), parses the response, and fail-safes correctly on HTTP errors and malformed JSON.

In state.ts, add a getRecentCycles(projectId, n) helper that returns the last N cycle results by reading PROGRESS.jsonl (cycle_end events only, newest first). Wire into the status --json output so the JSON includes a 'recent_cycles' array. Add tests.
Add a 'generalstaff history --verified-only' flag that filters out cycle_skipped and verification_failed rows from the history output. Works in both default and --format=compact modes. Add tests.

Improve session console output to include an ETA: after each cycle completes, print 'Cycle N completed ... (projected end: HH:MM)'. Projected end = now + (remaining cycles estimate * avg cycle duration). Skip ETA if fewer than 2 cycles have completed (need a sample). Add test.

Add a test for extractChangedFiles in cycle.ts that covers the --diff-filter=A added-only case: given a diff with only new files and no modifications, verify the function returns the added file paths correctly.

In src/audit.ts, add a function loadProgressEvents(projectId, filterFn) that returns a filtered array of parsed ProgressEntry objects from PROGRESS.jsonl. Use it to refactor existing readCycleEnds/summarizeCosts for consistency. Add tests covering filter matches, empty file, malformed lines.

Add a docs section to CLAUDE.md (Session context) documenting the OpenRouter reviewer opt-in (env vars GENERALSTAFF_REVIEWER_PROVIDER and OPENROUTER_API_KEY, where the key is sourced from on Ray's machines, how the .bat wrapper sets them). Update the memory-discipline note's cross-references.

In src/projects.ts, add a getProject(id) lookup helper with a typed 'not found' error that includes the list of available project ids. Refactor existing string-match fallbacks (like in cli.ts task list) to use it. Add tests.

Add a 'generalstaff digest --latest' flag that prints the absolute path of the most recently modified digest file in digests/, or a clear message if no digest exists. Add tests covering the happy path, empty directory, and filter compatibility with --json/--list.

Add a 'generalstaff summary --project=<id>' filter flag that restricts the fleet summary dashboard output to a single project. When unknown project id is passed, print a clear error listing valid ids (use getProject helper). Add tests.

Add a formatRelativeTime(iso: string, now?: Date) helper to src/format.ts that converts an ISO timestamp to a human relative string: 'just now' (<60s), '3 min ago', '2h ago', 'yesterday at HH:MM', 'N days ago'. Handles future dates gracefully ('in N min'). Include tests covering each bucket and invalid input.

Wire formatRelativeTime (from gs-085) into the 'generalstaff status' command output: replace the raw ISO timestamp on the 'Last cycle: ...' line with a relative version, but keep the ISO in parentheses for precision. Add tests. Only proceed after gs-085 lands.

Add a test for parseDigest in src/session.ts that verifies compatibility with the new multi-section digest format (## What got done / ## Issues / ## Details headers before the per-cycle sections). The existing cycle-section regex expects '## <project> — <cycle_id>'; confirm the new headers don't accidentally match.

Extract a shared readJsonl(path: string): unknown[] helper in src/audit.ts that reads a newline-delimited JSON file, skips empty lines, and silently drops malformed lines (returning the valid ones). Refactor existing tail/filter utilities to use it. Add tests covering missing file, empty file, malformed lines mixed with valid, and trailing newline.

Add 'generalstaff history --format=json' for machine-readable cycle history output. Emit an array of cycle objects with { cycle_id, project_id, started_at, outcome, diff_stats, reason }. Parse-able by external tools. Add tests.

Add a reviewer provider fallback chain. In src/reviewer.ts, if the primary provider (from GENERALSTAFF_REVIEWER_PROVIDER) returns a response starting with '[REVIEWER ERROR]', retry once using the provider named in GENERALSTAFF_REVIEWER_FALLBACK_PROVIDER (env var). Log the fallback attempt via appendProgress. Skip fallback if unset or equal to primary. Add tests for: fallback triggered, fallback not triggered (primary succeeded), fallback also fails, fallback equals primary (no retry).

Validate 'generalstaff task add' input: reject empty titles or whitespace-only titles with a clear error message ('task title cannot be empty') and non-zero exit. Add tests covering valid title, empty string, whitespace-only, and very long titles (accept but warn if over 500 chars).

Add a 'generalstaff log --grep=<pattern>' flag that filters log output by a case-insensitive regex pattern against the event+data fields of PROGRESS.jsonl. Rejects invalid regex with a clear error. Works alongside --lines/--project/--level. Add tests.

Add a formatFileCount(n: number) helper to src/format.ts that pluralizes: 0 -> '0 files', 1 -> '1 file', 2 -> '2 files'. Use it in writeDigest's 'What got done' list (replacing the current '${n} file(s)' template). Add tests covering 0, 1, 2, large numbers, and negative (should return '0 files').

Extract fetchCommitSubject from src/session.ts into a new module src/git.ts with its own tests. session.ts imports from there. The new module should be a natural home for future git helpers (countCommitsAhead currently lives in cycle.ts and could also move here in a later task).

Add a test for invokeOllamaReviewer verifying it correctly returns the message.content when the response also contains a 'thinking' field (some reasoning models like qwen3 populate both). Mock fetch to return { message: { content: 'real answer', thinking: 'internal reasoning' } } and verify the result is 'real answer' (thinking field ignored).

Add a 'generalstaff task count [--project=<id>]' subcommand that reports the number of pending vs done tasks per project (or for all projects when --project is omitted). Output format: 'myproj: 5 pending, 12 done (17 total)'. Add tests.

(BUG) Investigate why writeDigest's first cycle of a session sometimes falls back to cycle_id instead of the commit subject in the 'What got done' list. The 2026-04-17 morning run's digest showed cycle 1 as '20260417100252_4z21' instead of 'gs-087: add parseDigest tests' even though the commit landed cleanly. Reproduce with a git fixture: init repo, commit A, branch bot/work, commit B on bot/work, merge B into main, then call fetchCommitSubject(A, B) and expect B's subject. If the fixture test passes, instrument session.ts's cycle_start_sha / cycle_end_sha assignment for the first cycle — the bug may be that cycle 1's start SHA equals the session-init-commit SHA created at session start, and the cycle_end_sha reflects post-auto-merge state rather than the bot's own commit. Add regression test either way.

Add a 'generalstaff session --exclude-project=<id>[,<id>...]' filter. When the project picker (src/dispatcher.ts pickNextProject) evaluates candidates, skip any project id in the exclude set. Parse comma-separated values from the flag. Useful for 'skip this project for tonight's run'. Add tests covering: single exclude, multiple excludes, excluded project is the only eligible project (session ends with no-project), excluded project id doesn't exist (warn but don't error).

Add a 'session_complete' ProgressEntry event type (fleet-level, not per-project) that emits once at session end with aggregated stats: total_cycles, total_verified, total_failed, duration_minutes, stop_reason. Complements the existing per-project session_end entries. Add to ProgressEventType union in types.ts, emit from session.ts after the per-project session_end loop, and add tests verifying the event fires exactly once per session.

Add a 'generalstaff status --watch[=N]' flag that refreshes the status output every N seconds until killed (Ctrl-C). Default N=5 when --watch is specified without a value. Minimum N=1. Use setInterval and clear the terminal between renders (ANSI clear-screen, suppressed when not a TTY — fall back to repeated output). Add tests with a mocked timer covering: default interval, custom interval, interval clamping at 1.

Extract the result-categorization logic from src/session.ts (the verified/failed/skipped filtering used in the notification block and session-end logging) into a helper `categorizeResults(results: CycleResult[]): { verified: CycleResult[]; failed: CycleResult[]; skipped: CycleResult[] }` in a new src/results.ts module. verified_weak counts as verified (per existing convention). Refactor session.ts and src/summary.ts to use it. Add tests covering each bucket plus edge cases (empty array, all one type).

Include the reviewer provider name in the digest header so multi-provider runs are traceable from the digest alone. Read from GENERALSTAFF_REVIEWER_PROVIDER (default 'claude') at session start, thread it through writeDigest's config param, and emit a '**Reviewer:** <provider>' line in the digest header (e.g., '**Reviewer:** ollama (qwen3:8b)'). When GENERALSTAFF_REVIEWER_MODEL is set, include the model in parentheses. Add tests covering default, openrouter, ollama, and unknown-provider cases.

Add a pre-flight Ollama reachability check in session.ts when GENERALSTAFF_REVIEWER_PROVIDER=ollama. Before the first cycle runs, fire a GET to `${OLLAMA_HOST}/api/tags` (default http://localhost:11434). If unreachable, emit a warning to stderr suggesting the user either start the Ollama server (`ollama serve`) or set GENERALSTAFF_REVIEWER_FALLBACK_PROVIDER=openrouter. Non-fatal — the session proceeds; bad-call fallback will catch it at cycle time anyway, but the pre-flight warning gives clearer error attribution. Add tests mocking fetch for reachable/unreachable/timeout cases.

Include per-cycle engineer duration in the 'Cycle N completed' log line so users can spot slowdowns in real time. Change `Cycle N completed: generalstaff — verified (58m50s remaining)` to `Cycle N completed: generalstaff — verified (took 6m12s, 58m50s remaining)`. Compute duration from cycle.started_at and cycle.ended_at. Add test.

Remove the now-dead Telegram notify invocation from scripts/run_session.bat. src/notify.ts handles end-of-session notification directly from bun now (see 2026-04-17 notify refactor), so the powershell call in the .bat is redundant (and unreachable in detached launch contexts anyway). Keep scripts/notify_telegram.ps1 for manual re-sends. Update the .bat's comment block to reflect the new flow.

Add a test for invokeReviewerWithFallback covering the Ollama-unreachable-to-OpenRouter-fallback scenario: primary=ollama returns '[REVIEWER ERROR] Ollama fetch failed...', fallback=openrouter returns a well-formed response, verify final rawResponse is the openrouter content AND the onFallback callback fired with the primary error. Uses mocked fetch.

Enforce Hard Rule 5 (mandatory hands-off lists) with a pre-flight validator in src/projects.ts loadProjectsYaml: if any registered project has an empty or missing hands_off array, reject the config at load time with a clear error pointing at the offending project id and Rule 5. Add tests covering: valid config, missing hands_off, empty hands_off, multiple projects where one is invalid.

Add test coverage for the session_complete ProgressEntry event (introduced by gs-099): verify exactly one event fires per session_end path, data.total_cycles matches allResults.length, data.stop_reason is in the expected set ('budget'|'max-cycles'|'stop-file'|'no-project'|'insufficient-budget'|'empty-cycles'), data.duration_minutes is a non-negative number. Use a session fixture or mock.

Produce a README outline proposal at `docs/README-PROPOSAL.md` following the matiassingers/awesome-readme checklist structure. Read README.md (existing), CLAUDE.md, DESIGN.md, PIVOT-2026-04-15.md, FUTURE-DIRECTIONS-2026-04-15.md, RULE-RELAXATION-2026-04-15.md, UI-VISION-2026-04-15.md, and the PHASE-1-*-2026-04-15 docs. Output section headers + one-bullet-per-section summary + cite which source doc each bullet draws from. Do NOT modify README.md itself. Do NOT write any narrative prose in the sections. Only propose structure and what-belongs-where. This is pure retrieval/outlining on behalf of the README dogfooding experiment proposed in FUTURE-DIRECTIONS §6 — the user will handle voice, positioning, and final prose in a later interactive session. Create the docs/ directory if it doesn't exist.

Augment docs/README-PROPOSAL.md with voice-calibration annotations. Read VOICE.md in full (all sections including 'Mainstream-economics anchors' and 'Dogfooding as credibility'). For each proposed section in README-PROPOSAL.md, append a one-line `_Voice:_` annotation that flags where the editorial voice calibration applies — e.g., section §5 'The problem' → '_Voice: lean into anti-slop + anti-extraction framing from VOICE.md §1; optional Falk & Tsoukalas anchor._'; section §11 'Why this over the alternatives' → '_Voice: direct Polsia comparison allowed per VOICE.md §1; keep pointed not screed per §2; dogfooding contrast is structural not rhetorical per VOICE.md §Dogfooding._'; section §13 'Hammerstein framing' → '_Voice: the Marxist-compatible reading per VOICE.md §Intellectual framing is fair game._'; section §3 'Status / Maturity' → '_Voice: add dogfooding credibility line per VOICE.md §Dogfooding README placement._'; section §6 'The approach / How it works' → '_Voice: reference PROGRESS.jsonl as observable evidence per VOICE.md §Dogfooding README placement._'; neutral sections (badges, prerequisites, license) → '_Voice: neutral technical._'. Do NOT rewrite any section headers or bullets. Do NOT write the actual README prose. Only add the inline `_Voice:_` lines.

(BUG) When the engineer subprocess exits abnormally (exit code null, meaning killed by signal, OR any non-zero exit code), the cycle should skip the verification gate + reviewer entirely and mark itself verification_failed with reason 'engineer exited abnormally (code=X)'. Currently src/cycle.ts proceeds through verification on whatever partial diff exists, which accepted a killed-mid-task engineer's partial work as verified on 2026-04-17 cycle 10. Add early-return in executeCycle after engineer completes if engineerResult.exitCode === null || engineerResult.exitCode !== 0. Add tests covering: exit=0 (proceeds), exit=1 (blocks), exit=null i.e. killed (blocks), with verification of the cycle_end event's reason field.

Add a session-level watchdog that logs a warning when a cycle takes >2x its cycle_budget_minutes. Purpose: give users a visible signal that an engineer or reviewer step is stuck before the engineer timeout fires. Implementation: in session.ts, after executeCycle returns, compute actual duration from result.started_at / result.ended_at and compare to project.cycle_budget_minutes * 2. Emit console.error('[WATCHDOG] cycle X took Nm, 2x the budget — consider investigating') when threshold exceeded. Non-fatal, purely observability. Add test.

Add `generalstaff digest --regen=<digest-file-path>` that re-renders an existing digest markdown from the underlying PROGRESS.jsonl cycle_end events. Useful for recovering when a digest rendered with a bug (like the 2026-04-17 cycle-1 subject fallback) needs a fresh pass with the corrected code. Reads the digest file's date range, finds matching cycle_end entries, rebuilds CycleResult objects, calls writeDigest. Writes to a new timestamped file rather than overwriting. Add tests.

Add a regression test for the engineer timeout Windows process-tree kill (src/engineer.ts killChildTree). Since testing the actual spawn+kill path is cross-platform-flaky in a unit test, instead assert that on process.platform === 'win32', the code path uses spawnSync('taskkill', ['/pid', ..., '/f', '/t']) rather than child.kill('SIGTERM'). Mock spawnSync and verify the call shape. Also verify that on non-Windows platforms the signal path is used.

Add end-to-end test coverage for notifySessionEnd's real homedir loader path (currently only the dependency-injection path is tested in tests/notify.test.ts). Use a fixture ~/.claude layout inside a tmpdir, point HOME / USERPROFILE env at it, verify loadTelegramCredentials reads the correct files. Restore env after. Add a cross-platform skip marker if HOME env manipulation is too brittle on Windows CI.

Investigate and fix `.bot-worktree` cleanup after abnormal cycle termination. When a cycle is killed (engineer timeout, manual taskkill, STOP file), does cleanupWorktree() still fire? If not, stale worktrees accumulate and the next cycle's `git worktree add` fails. Add a pre-cycle cleanup pass that removes any existing `.bot-worktree` before `git worktree add`. Add tests covering: clean run (no stale worktree), stale worktree present (removed + replaced), worktree locked by another process (warn but don't crash).

Add a `generalstaff doctor --fix` mode. Currently `doctor` only diagnoses; the `--fix` mode prompts (y/N) before each auto-resolvable issue: missing state directories → create, stale `.bot-worktree` → remove, orphaned STOP file from crashed session → remove (with confirmation). Never touches user code or git refs. Add tests for each fix path using fixture project roots.

Add validateConfig(yaml) pre-flight in src/projects.ts that runs BEFORE a session starts and rejects invalid configs with a clear error listing every problem at once (don't fail on first). Checks: project paths exist, hands_off non-empty (Hard Rule 5), engineer_command set, verification_command set, cycle_budget_minutes > 0, branch name valid. Emit errors to stderr with the project id prefix. Add tests for each failure mode.

Add a `generalstaff stop --force` subcommand that writes the STOP file AND attempts to kill any running bun/claude processes associated with the current session. On Windows, use `taskkill /pid X /f /t` matching PIDs from running sessions (read from state if tracked). Current `stop` just writes the file, which doesn't interrupt a hung cycle. Add tests using mocked process table.

Add test coverage for parseReviewerResponse when the response contains `<think>...</think>` tags before the JSON (Qwen3 reasoning mode can emit these in Ollama responses). Expected behavior: strip the think-tags or scan past them, then parse the trailing JSON. Add fixtures covering: think-tags wrapping reasoning with JSON after, nested think-tags, think-tags that contain JSON-looking text that should NOT be parsed.

Refactor executeCycle in src/cycle.ts to consolidate its multiple early-return paths into a single assembly point. Currently there are 6+ places that construct a CycleResult with different fields set, which makes adding new fields (like the gs-111 abnormal-exit block) error-prone. Approach: use a mutable builder object throughout the function, single return at the end. Don't change behavior — just structure. Safety: the 600+ tests are the regression guard. Add no new tests; this is a pure refactor.

Improve the 'bot/work branch has unmerged commits' error message in src/cycle.ts to interpolate the resolved project path in the suggested git command — e.g., `git -C /full/path/to/project merge --no-ff bot/work` rather than the current placeholder. Also suggest the alternative `auto_merge: true` config change with the exact project id. Add a test.

Add a `generalstaff session --verbose` flag that streams PROGRESS.jsonl events to stdout as they fire (not just the cycle-completion summaries). Useful for debugging stuck cycles in real time and for demo screencasts. Format each event as one line with timestamp + event name + short data summary. Default off. Add test.

Investigate and fix scripts/wait_then_launch_ollama.ps1. Used on 2026-04-17 for the first dentist-run chain attempt — it polled correctly but never fired the child Start-Process when the idle threshold was met. Reproduce with a fixture log directory + short threshold, determine root cause, fix, add smoke test.

Design doc: the session-budget vs cycle-budget reservation math. A 60-min session only allows 25 usable min of cycles (60 - 35-min reservation). Document in DESIGN.md. Propose one of: (a) lower cycle_budget_minutes since real cycles are 4-8 min, (b) adapt based on moving-average cycle duration, (c) document as-is. No code.

Add `generalstaff session --chain=<N>` flag that queues N back-to-back sessions, each starting after the previous finishes. Same budget/reviewer across children. Enables unattended multi-hour runs past the 10-cycle-per-session cap. Add tests for --chain=0 (error), --chain=1 (equivalent to no flag), --chain=3.
Add `generalstaff status --sessions` flag: table of last N sessions (start time, duration, cycle count, pass/fail, reviewer, stop reason). Reads session_complete PROGRESS events. Default N=10. Tests.

Add `generalstaff log --since=<ts>` filter (alongside --lines/--level/--grep). Accepts absolute ISO or relative forms like '1h'/'30m'/'2d'. Tests covering each form plus malformed and future timestamps.
Add test coverage for src/notify.ts truncateForTelegram: length always <= 3900, marker present, UTF-8 multi-byte chars at boundary don't produce invalid bytes (em-dashes/emoji edge cases).
Add `generalstaff cycle-redo --project=<id> --task=<id>` to reopen a done task as pending. Tests: reopen-done, reopen-pending (noop), reopen-not-found.

Add mid-cycle STOP file detection. Currently isStopFilePresent() is only checked at cycle boundaries. Use fs.watch on the STOP path in session.ts to SIGTERM the active engineer + mark cycle_skipped when triggered. Test with a test-mode synchronous watcher.

(BUG) When a cycle's reviewer_verdict is verification_failed, the commit the engineer made on bot/work is NOT rolled back, and the next cycle's auto_merge path (cycle.ts:417) or session-end auto_merge (session.ts:370-376, whose comment falsely asserts 'anything on bot/work here has already passed the per-cycle verification gate') merges it into HEAD anyway. On 2026-04-17 afternoon this path landed 6 hands-off-violating and scope-creeping commits on master after they had been reported verification_failed. Fix: in src/cycle.ts executeCycle, after the reviewer returns a non-verified outcome WITH a non-empty diff, reset bot/work back to the cycle's start_sha so the bad commit is discarded and not later auto-merged. Log a cycle_rollback PROGRESS event (add to ProgressEventType union + VALID_EVENTS in src/types.ts) naming before_sha and after_sha. Update the optimistic comment in session.ts:370-376 to reflect the new guarantee. Add tests in tests/cycle.test.ts covering: (a) verified cycle — no rollback, (b) verification_failed with diff — rollback fires, cycle_end's end_sha equals start_sha, (c) verification_failed with empty diff — no rollback (noop), (d) verified_weak — no rollback (still considered acceptable work). Do NOT modify src/safety.ts or src/reviewer.ts; the fix lives entirely in src/cycle.ts, src/session.ts, src/types.ts.

(BUG) Reviewer producing false-positive hands_off_violations. On 2026-04-17's second session (Ollama qwen3:8b), 3 of 6 failed cycles had reviewer-reported hands_off_violations naming files that were NOT in the diff — e.g., gs-131 (cycle juzs) reported ['src/safety.ts','src/reviewer.ts','src/prompts/'] as violations but the diff_summary shows the bot only touched active_engineer.ts/cycle.ts/engineer.ts/session.ts/stop_watcher.ts and their tests. Cycles eosu (gs-115) and ek00 (gs-121) had the same pattern. These false positives wasted ~25 minutes of session budget. Fix: in src/cycle.ts after the reviewer verdict returns, cross-reference reviewer-reported hands_off_violations against the actual changed-files list (already computed upstream via extractChangedFiles). Drop any violation whose named file/pattern does not match any file in the diff (reuse matchesHandsOff's pattern-matching semantics for parity). If dropping violations leaves no real violations AND no other failure signals (scope_drift_files, silent_failures), flip the verdict from verification_failed to verified. Emit a 'reviewer_hallucination' PROGRESS event (add to ProgressEventType + VALID_EVENTS in src/types.ts) naming the dropped file list for observability, regardless of whether the verdict flipped. Add tests in tests/cycle.test.ts covering: (a) reviewer reports a file IS in the diff — violation stands, (b) reviewer reports a file NOT in the diff — violation dropped, hallucination event emitted, (c) all violations hallucinated AND no other failures — verdict flips to verified, (d) mixed real + hallucinated — real stands, hallucinated dropped. Do NOT modify src/reviewer.ts — the sanity-check layer lives in cycle.ts.

(BUG) In src/types.ts VALID_EVENTS is missing 'worktree_preflight' even though the ProgressEventType union includes it and src/cycle.ts:394,399 actively emits that event. isProgressEntry() therefore rejects every worktree_preflight entry as malformed, and those entries silently disappear from loadProgressEvents/tailProgressLog/getRecentCycles/loadRecentSessions. Add 'worktree_preflight' to VALID_EVENTS and extend tests/types.test.ts's 'accepts all valid event types' list so the omission can't silently recur. Also add a regression test that feeds an object with event='worktree_preflight' to isProgressEntry and asserts true.

Refactor src/cycle.ts runSingleCycle to use getProject + ProjectNotFoundError instead of the legacy findProject lookup (which returns undefined and prints a bare 'Project X not found' line). Mirror the pattern the summary/bot-status commands in src/cli.ts adopted in gs-082: print 'Error: project <id> not found' then 'Available: <ids>' when known ids exist, then process.exit(1). Add a test in tests/cycle.test.ts using a fake yaml that has no matching project and verify the error + exit code.

Add a test in tests/session.test.ts covering formatSessionPlanPreview from src/session.ts. Exercise: (a) empty plan (total_cycles=0) returns the 'No cycles fit in the budget.' line; (b) single-project plan formats the Project/Cycles header with correct padding; (c) multiple projects with differing id widths (short 'x' alongside 'generalstaff') align under a width-matched header. Asserts the literal line structure so future column-layout regressions are caught.

Add test coverage for preflightCleanupWorktree in src/cycle.ts. Build a tmp project dir fixture with a fake .bot-worktree folder and exercise the three branches: (a) no stale worktree returns {wasStale:false,removed:false}, (b) stale worktree that is removable returns {wasStale:true,removed:true}, (c) stale worktree where the injected rmFn throws returns {wasStale:true,removed:false,warning:<message includes 'locked by another process'>}. Use the rmFn param so the test is deterministic on Windows and Unix.

Add test coverage for checkCycleWatchdog in src/session.ts. Cases: (a) cycleBudgetMinutes <= 0 returns null even for large durations, (b) duration exactly at threshold (2 * budget * 60) returns null (boundary — equal is NOT a warning), (c) duration one second over the threshold returns a warning string containing the formatted duration and budget number, (d) very large duration (e.g. 3x budget) still returns a single-line string. Prevents silent drift if someone changes WATCHDOG_MULTIPLIER without updating tests.

Add --dry-run to src/clean.ts runClean so operators can preview exactly what would be deleted before committing. Flag threads through from cli.ts clean command (existing --keep/--log-days are parsed there). In dry-run mode: enumerate each worktree/log/cycle that would be removed with a '[DRY RUN] Would remove: <path>' line, do not invoke rmSync or git worktree remove, and report the same 'Cleaned N item(s)' footer prefixed with '[DRY RUN] '. Add tests in tests/clean.test.ts covering each of the three cleanup phases.

Extract a shared botWorktreePath(project) helper into src/state.ts (or a new lightweight src/paths.ts — your call) and refactor the three current inline uses: src/cycle.ts worktreePath (currently a private function), src/clean.ts join(p.path, '.bot-worktree'), and src/doctor.ts join(p.path, '.bot-worktree'). Keep the same returned string so no behavior changes. Add a trivial test pinning the shape ('<path>/.bot-worktree'). Do NOT touch src/safety.ts (hands-off) — its inline join stays.

Add a 'generalstaff task rm --project=<id> --task=<task-id>' subcommand in src/cli.ts that deletes a task from tasks.json. Mirrors task done/cycle-redo error handling: print 'Error: task X not found' with Available: list, 'Error: no tasks file for project Y' when the project state dir is missing. Implement removeTask in src/tasks.ts returning the same discriminated-union kind as markTaskDone. Include tests for success, task-not-found, project-not-found, and verify the file is rewritten without the removed entry (other tasks preserved).

Add --priority=N filter to 'generalstaff task list' (cli.ts) so operators can say 'task list --project=myapp --priority=1' to see only P1 tasks. Parse N as integer, error with 'Error: --priority must be a positive integer' on bad input. Add tests for filter match, empty filter result (prints 'No pending tasks at priority N.'), and mixing with no filter (existing behavior preserved).

Add test coverage for countRemainingWork / hasMoreWork in src/work_detection.ts covering the 'unknown mode' fallback — pass a ProjectConfig with work_detection set to a value outside the VALID_WORK_DETECTION set (cast through unknown) and assert hasMoreWork returns false and countRemainingWork returns 0 (the fail-safe branches). Current tests exercise each valid mode but not the default-case safety net, so a regression there would silently enable chaining for misconfigured projects.

Add test coverage for isNoopCommand in src/verification.ts (currently only reached indirectly via runVerification). Cover: 'true', ':', 'echo', 'exit 0' all return true; 'true && npm test' returns true (startsWith-match per current implementation, document whether that's intentional); 'tree', 'truecheck' (begins with 'true' but not a word boundary) — current code treats 'truecheck' as noop because startsWith(noop + ' ') requires space, so 'truecheck' is NOT a noop — add the test pinning that behavior; whitespace-only returns false. Because isNoopCommand is module-private, either export it for tests or exercise it through runVerification with dryRun=true (which calls it explicitly). Prefer exporting.

Improve src/init.ts scaffolding: accept a --priority=N flag (default 2) and emit it into the YAML snippet and into a first 'pending' template task written to tasks.json (instead of the current empty-array content). The template task title should read 'Describe first task for <project_id> (edit me or run generalstaff task rm)' with status pending and the given priority. Rationale: a fresh project with zero tasks produces 'no more work' on the first hasMoreWork check, so the bot never runs until the user hand-edits tasks.json. Wire through from cli.ts init. Add tests covering default priority, explicit priority, and the seeded task's shape.

(SUPERSEDED by gs-165) Original spec required exporting stopFilePath from src/safety.ts (hands-off); 4 attempts in 2026-04-17 chain session 3 were cleanly rolled back by gs-132's new rollback logic. Replaced with gs-165 which uses inline STOP path construction (same pattern as stop_watcher.ts + session.ts already use). Kept as historical record.

Add a 'generalstaff log --tail' alias flag that is equivalent to --lines=9999 — convenience for 'show me everything' without counting. Parse flag in cli.ts log command, when set override the parsed --lines value. If both --tail and --lines=N are passed, error 'Error: --tail and --lines are mutually exclusive'. Add tests covering each combination and verify filter flags (--level, --grep, --since) still compose with --tail.

Add a 'generalstaff history --outcome=<verified|verified_weak|verification_failed|cycle_skipped>' filter in src/cli.ts history command (alongside existing --verified-only). Threads into loadCycleHistory / loadCycleHistoryJson as an extra option (src/audit.ts collectCycleEnds). Error 'Error: unknown --outcome value' with the valid list when passed a bad value. If both --verified-only and --outcome are set, error 'Error: --verified-only and --outcome are mutually exclusive'. Add tests covering each valid value, invalid value, and mutual exclusion.

Fact-extraction research note. Append a dated section '## 2026-04-17 — pi-autoresearch + karpathy/autoresearch mechanisms' to research-notes.md documenting two mechanisms directly relevant to GeneralStaff Phase 2 design: (a) pi-autoresearch's CONFIDENCE SCORING (session noise floor, 2.0x threshold for 'real' improvement, green/yellow/red ranges) — extract the exact formula and how session noise is computed; (b) pi-autoresearch's AUTO-REVERT-ON-REGRESSION mechanism — extract what triggers a revert, what metric is measured, how state is restored. Also briefly summarize karpathy/autoresearch as the canonical inspiration and note what pi-autoresearch added on top. Use WebFetch on https://github.com/davebcn87/pi-autoresearch and https://github.com/karpathy/autoresearch. PURE FACT EXTRACTION — NO editorializing, NO recommendations, NO opinion, NO proposals for how to apply. Docs-only task: no code changes, no new tests. research-notes.md is NOT in the hands-off list, so this is safe. Verification will pass as a no-code diff.

Create src/providers/types.ts defining the provider abstraction interfaces: LLMProvider (name: string, invoke(prompt: string, opts?: { timeoutMs?: number; maxTokens?: number; temperature?: number }): Promise<{ content: string; error?: string }>), ProviderRole ('digest' | 'cycle_summary' | 'classifier' — explicitly NOT including 'reviewer' since reviewer dispatch is handled inline in src/reviewer.ts per Phase 2 scoping), ProviderHealth ({ reachable: boolean; host?: string; error?: string; latencyMs?: number }), and ProviderDescriptor ({ id: string; kind: 'ollama' | 'openrouter' | 'claude'; model: string; host?: string; api_key_env?: string }). All pure type definitions with no runtime code. Add tests in tests/providers.types.test.ts exercising only the type shape (e.g. satisfies checks) — no runtime assertions needed beyond importing the module without errors. Do NOT import from src/reviewer.ts — the new abstraction lives alongside, not on top of, reviewer dispatch.

Create src/providers/ollama.ts exporting createOllamaProvider(descriptor: ProviderDescriptor): LLMProvider that returns an object conforming to the LLMProvider interface from gs-150. invoke() posts to <host>/api/chat (mirror the shape already used by invokeOllamaReviewer in src/reviewer.ts) — model from descriptor.model, host defaults to http://localhost:11434, options.temperature defaults to 0, options.maxTokens maps to num_predict (default 800 — much smaller than reviewer's 8000 since digest-tier tasks are one-shot summaries). On HTTP non-2xx or fetch error return { content: '', error: '<message>' } rather than the [REVIEWER ERROR] sentinel — this path is NOT reviewer-shaped. Include a health() method returning ProviderHealth via a GET <host>/api/tags probe (reuse logic equivalent to src/ollama.ts checkOllamaReachable). Add tests in tests/providers.ollama.test.ts mocking globalThis.fetch: (a) happy path returns content, (b) non-2xx returns error, (c) fetch throws returns error, (d) host with trailing slash is normalized, (e) health() reachable=true on 200 from /api/tags, (f) health() reachable=false on fetch failure. Do NOT modify src/reviewer.ts or src/ollama.ts.

Create src/providers/registry.ts with loadProviderRegistry(configPath?: string) that reads provider_config.yaml from the project root (default path getRootDir() + '/provider_config.yaml') and returns { providers: Map<string, ProviderDescriptor>, routes: Record<ProviderRole, string> }. Use the yaml package (already a dep per src/projects.ts). Validate: each provider entry has id, kind in {'ollama','openrouter','claude'}, model string, host optional. routes entry keys must be in {'digest','cycle_summary','classifier'}; values must reference a providers key. On malformed input throw a ProviderConfigError (new subclass of Error) naming the field and the offending value. getProviderForRole(registry, role): LLMProvider returns the instantiated provider for that role (dispatch on kind — only ollama implemented in Phase 2; openrouter/claude kinds throw 'not implemented in Phase 2 — see src/reviewer.ts for inline dispatch'). If provider_config.yaml is missing, return a registry with no providers and routes all pointing at 'noop' — callers should check with hasProviderForRole() before dispatching. Add tests in tests/providers.registry.test.ts covering valid config, missing file (returns empty registry), malformed (each required-field case throws ProviderConfigError), unknown kind rejected, unreferenced route rejected. Do NOT modify src/reviewer.ts or projects.yaml.

Create provider_config.yaml.example at repo root documenting the Phase 2 schema. Mirror projects.yaml.example style — heavy comments explaining each field, rationale for defaults, a warning that reviewer routing does NOT go here (it's controlled via GENERALSTAFF_REVIEWER_PROVIDER env var as of Phase 1). Structure: a top-level 'providers:' map with one ollama entry (id: ollama_llama3, kind: ollama, model: llama3:8b, host: http://localhost:11434) and a 'routes:' block mapping digest/cycle_summary/classifier to ollama_llama3. Include a comment block at top stating the file is NOT hands-off (bots can edit it) and pointing to FUTURE-DIRECTIONS §2 tier taxonomy for the rationale. Also create tests/providers.example.test.ts that loads this file via loadProviderRegistry from gs-152 and asserts the registry parses without error and routes resolve — keeps the example file from rotting away from the loader. Do NOT create provider_config.yaml (the real one) — that's user setup, not a task deliverable.

Create src/digest_llm.ts with generateDigestNarrative(results: CycleResult[], durationMinutes: number, provider: LLMProvider): Promise<{ narrative: string; fellBack: boolean; error?: string }>. Build a compact prompt (~300 tokens) summarizing cycles: verified count, failed count, one-line-per-verified-cycle with the commit subject (use fetchCommitSubject from src/git.ts — already imported in session.ts), one-line-per-failed-cycle with the reason. Prompt asks for a 2-4 sentence plain-English narrative of what happened this session — no bullets, no headings, just prose. Invoke the provider with maxTokens=200, temperature=0. On provider error or empty content, return { narrative: '', fellBack: true, error } so callers can fall back to the existing templated line. On success return { narrative: content.trim(), fellBack: false }. Add tests in tests/digest_llm.test.ts using a stub LLMProvider: (a) happy path returns narrative, (b) provider returns empty content triggers fellBack, (c) provider returns error triggers fellBack, (d) narrative is trimmed. Do NOT modify src/session.ts in this task — wiring happens in gs-158.

Create src/cycle_summary_llm.ts with generateCycleDescription(result: CycleResult, diffStat: string, provider: LLMProvider): Promise<{ description: string; fellBack: boolean; error?: string }>. Build a tiny prompt (~150 tokens) from cycle_id, project_id, final_outcome, diff_stats, and diff_stat summary; ask for a single-line (max ~80 char) human-readable description of what the cycle did. Currently the digest's 'What got done' section uses fetchCommitSubject in writeDigest; this new function produces a richer one-liner that can optionally replace it. Provider invoked with maxTokens=80, temperature=0. Strip newlines from output so it stays single-line; truncate at 120 chars with ellipsis. On error return fellBack=true. Add tests in tests/cycle_summary_llm.test.ts with a stub provider: (a) happy path returns description, (b) provider strips internal newlines, (c) truncates oversize output, (d) error path fellBack=true. Do NOT wire into writeDigest in this task — that's gs-158's job behind a feature flag.

Create tests/providers.calibration.test.ts — a dual-run reviewer-calibration harness that takes an array of fixture diffs (create 3 minimal fixtures under tests/fixtures/reviewer_calibration/: verified_simple (small tests-added diff), hands_off_violation (touches src/safety.ts), verification_failed_scope_drift (diff unrelated to any stated task)). For each fixture, invoke invokeReviewerProvider from src/reviewer.ts TWICE: once with provider='claude' (skipped when CLAUDE_BINARY env not present — the test prints 'skip: no claude binary' and passes), once with provider='ollama' (skipped when Ollama is unreachable via checkOllamaReachable — also prints skip and passes). Parse both responses via parseReviewerResponse and assert the verdicts AGREE on each fixture (verified/verified, verification_failed/verification_failed). On disagreement, fail with a diff of the two responses. This is a GATE test for Phase 2 tier taxonomy — catches Ollama drifting from Claude on straightforward cases. Marked skip-when-unavailable so CI without local Ollama/claude still passes. Fixtures live under tests/fixtures/reviewer_calibration/ (new subdir). Do NOT modify src/reviewer.ts.

Add 'generalstaff providers list' subcommand in src/cli.ts. When invoked, load provider_config.yaml via loadProviderRegistry from gs-152. For each configured provider, print: id, kind, model, host (if ollama), api_key_env presence (openrouter, but noted as unimplemented in Phase 2). Support --json flag emitting the full registry as JSON. If provider_config.yaml is missing, print 'No provider_config.yaml found. See provider_config.yaml.example for format.' and exit 0 (not an error — the file is optional). Also list each route (digest/cycle_summary/classifier) with its resolved provider id, or '(unrouted)' if route not configured. Add tests in tests/cli.test.ts (or extend) covering: (a) missing file message, (b) valid file table output, (c) --json output shape. Wire into the switch(command) block and into printUsage()'s help text. Do NOT touch reviewer routing output — that's controlled by GENERALSTAFF_REVIEWER_PROVIDER env var and already shown via 'generalstaff config'.

Wire generateDigestNarrative (gs-154) into writeDigest in src/session.ts behind a feature flag. When env var GENERALSTAFF_DIGEST_NARRATIVE_PROVIDER is set to a non-empty provider id AND provider_config.yaml exists AND the registry resolves that id, instantiate the provider via getProviderForRole, call generateDigestNarrative, and if it returns fellBack=false inject the narrative as a new '**Narrative:**' line in the digest front-matter (right after '**Summary:**'). If fellBack=true OR the env var is unset, writeDigest produces exactly the current output (templated only). The feature must default OFF — no behavior change for existing users until they opt in. Log a digest_narrative_generated PROGRESS event to the _fleet log (follow-up: actually ADDING this event type to ProgressEventType is gs-161; until then just console.log the fallback status, no PROGRESS event). Add tests in tests/session.test.ts: (a) no env var → identical to baseline writeDigest output, (b) env var set + provider returns narrative → digest contains '**Narrative:**' line, (c) env var set + provider errors → narrative line absent, digest otherwise complete. Do NOT modify projects.yaml or the reviewer dispatch.

Add 'generalstaff providers ping <provider-id>' subcommand in src/cli.ts. Loads the registry via loadProviderRegistry, instantiates the named provider, calls its health() method (gs-151 defines this on the ollama provider; other kinds throw 'not implemented in Phase 2'). Prints: 'Provider <id>: reachable (<latencyMs>ms)' on success, 'Provider <id>: unreachable — <error>' on failure (exit code 1 on failure for scripting). --json flag dumps the ProviderHealth object. Also support 'generalstaff providers ping --all' which pings every configured provider in parallel and prints a summary table. Add tests in tests/cli.test.ts covering: unknown provider id errors, ping single provider success, ping single provider failure (exit 1), --all table. Wire into the switch(command) block and printUsage() help text. Do NOT touch src/reviewer.ts; the existing 'generalstaff doctor' command has Ollama-specific probes — leave those as-is.

Add test coverage in tests/session.test.ts for graceful degradation when GENERALSTAFF_DIGEST_NARRATIVE_PROVIDER is set but the underlying provider is unreachable. Scenario: set env var to 'ollama_llama3', provide a test provider_config.yaml fixture, stub the ollama provider's invoke() to return an error, run writeDigest — assert: (a) the session completes without throwing, (b) the digest file is written, (c) the digest contains the standard '**Summary:**' line and does NOT contain '**Narrative:**', (d) an appropriate console.log warning is emitted. Also add an integration-style test that the existing pre-flight Ollama reachability warning in runSession still prints when GENERALSTAFF_REVIEWER_PROVIDER=ollama and the server is down — regression guard since gs-158 wiring touches the same code area. Do NOT modify src/reviewer.ts or projects.yaml. No changes to src/ollama.ts needed — existing checkOllamaReachable is sufficient.

Add 'provider_invoked' and 'provider_fallback' entries to ProgressEventType union AND VALID_EVENTS runtime list in src/types.ts (mirror the pattern gs-134 fixed for worktree_preflight — both lists must match or isProgressEntry silently rejects). provider_invoked fires when any non-reviewer provider is invoked (initially only from src/digest_llm.ts and src/cycle_summary_llm.ts); data shape { provider_id: string, role: 'digest'|'cycle_summary'|'classifier', prompt_length: number, duration_ms: number, success: boolean }. provider_fallback fires when a provider fails and the caller falls back to the templated path; data shape { provider_id: string, role: string, error: string }. Add a test in tests/types.test.ts extending the 'accepts all valid event types' list so omission can't silently recur. Do NOT emit these events from src/reviewer.ts — the reviewer already has its own reviewer_invoked / reviewer_fallback events; those stay as-is per Phase 2 scoping. Actually emitting the new events from digest_llm.ts / cycle_summary_llm.ts is a follow-up (can land in the same task or as a trivial follow-on — draft handles this as a one-line appendProgress call site if time permits).

Add test coverage in tests/providers.registry.test.ts for every malformed-input branch of loadProviderRegistry (gs-152). Cases: (a) yaml parse failure (raw '{[ bad'), (b) top-level providers not a map, (c) provider entry missing id, (d) provider entry missing kind, (e) provider entry with unknown kind ('gemini' — not in Phase 2), (f) provider entry missing model, (g) routes referencing a provider id that is not declared, (h) routes with unknown role key, (i) empty file (returns empty registry, not an error — this is the 'no provider_config.yaml' equivalent for an existing-but-blank file). Each case asserts either a ProviderConfigError with the expected field name OR the documented lenient fallback. Also add a fixture at tests/fixtures/provider_config_malformed/ with one bad example per case. Do NOT touch provider_config.yaml.example — that stays as the golden-path reference.

Add docs/provider-config-format.md documenting the provider_config.yaml schema (separate from the example file so long-form explanation doesn't clutter the inline comments). Sections: (1) Purpose — why provider_config.yaml exists alongside the reviewer's env-var-based dispatch, citing FUTURE-DIRECTIONS §2 tier taxonomy. (2) Schema reference — each top-level block (providers, routes) with required/optional fields and types. (3) Supported provider kinds — ollama (fully implemented), openrouter/claude (listed but Phase 2 defers implementation to reviewer's inline path). (4) Role definitions — digest, cycle_summary, classifier; what each one controls. (5) A complete worked example copied from provider_config.yaml.example. (6) FAQ: 'Why isn't reviewer routing here?' (because reviewer dispatch stays in src/reviewer.ts this phase). Target length ~200 lines. Do NOT place under docs/internal/ (hands-off) or docs/sessions/ (hands-off) — lands in docs/ root which is NOT hands-off. Add a link to this doc from docs/ index if one exists (check first; create if not). No tests needed for prose-only docs.

Re-implement gs-146 (stop --status / --check) WITHOUT modifying src/safety.ts. In src/cli.ts's stop command handler, add a --status flag (and --check as alias) that is mutually exclusive with --force. When set: construct the STOP path inline via pathJoin(getRootDir(), 'STOP') — same pattern used in src/stop_watcher.ts and src/session.ts (see the 'workaround' comment near session.ts's STOP usage — stopFilePath is module-private in safety.ts and that's deliberate; do NOT export it). Check existsSync(stopPath); print 'STOP file: present at <path>' or 'STOP file: absent'. Read state/session.pid if it exists (readFileSync utf8, trim); print 'Session pid: <pid>' or 'Session pid: none recorded'. Do not create or remove anything — read-only inspection. Tests in tests/cli.test.ts covering: stop-present+pid, stop-present+nopid, stop-absent+pid, stop-absent+nopid, --status-with-force errors out. Wire into printUsage() help text. Do NOT modify src/safety.ts — it is hands-off and was the blocker for the original gs-146 (4 failed attempts in 2026-04-17 session 3). gs-132's rollback caught all 4 attempts cleanly; this task's purpose is to unblock the feature via a correct implementation.

(PHASE 3 BLOCKER) Align tasks.json path handling between src/work_detection.ts and src/cycle.ts. Currently src/work_detection.ts:71 and :119 (greenfieldCountRemaining + greenfieldHasMoreWork) read from `${getRootDir()}/state/<projectId>/tasks.json` (inside GeneralStaff's repo), while src/cycle.ts:326 (detectMarkedDoneTasks) reads diff from `${project.path}/state/<projectId>/tasks.json` (inside the managed project's repo). For dogfood (project.path == getRootDir()) they coincide; for any non-dogfood project (like gamr) they diverge and the dispatcher cannot function. Fix: change greenfieldCountRemaining and greenfieldHasMoreWork signatures to accept a ProjectConfig (or projectPath + projectId) instead of just projectId, and read from `${project.path}/state/<projectId>/tasks.json`. Update all callers (likely src/session.ts, src/summary.ts, src/work_detection.ts internal dispatch, anywhere else grep finds). Add a test in tests/work_detection.test.ts specifically using a project.path that differs from getRootDir() to prove the fix. The dogfood case still works because generalstaff's project.path IS the GeneralStaff repo root. Background: discovered 2026-04-17 evening during first gamr bootstrap setup — see research-notes.md '2026-04-17 — Phase 3 state-path architectural finding' for full context. This fix unblocks Phase 3 entirely.

After gs-166 lands, update src/bootstrap.ts to write state/<id>/tasks.json inside the TARGET project directory, not inside GeneralStaff's state/ dir. Specifically: the tasks.json written by runBootstrap should go to `${targetDir}/state/<projectId>/tasks.json` so it's committed alongside the target project's code and the dispatcher can read it via project.path lookup. Update generateProposal (and possibly tests) to reflect the new location. The .generalstaff-proposal/ staging dir still contains an idea.md + CLAUDE-AUTONOMOUS.md draft + hands_off.yaml proposal; tasks.json moves to the canonical ${targetDir}/state/ location. README-PROPOSAL.md should be updated to explain the new layout. Update tests/bootstrap.test.ts accordingly. Depends on gs-166.

Add an integration test that exercises a FULL non-dogfood cycle end-to-end with a tmp fixture project. Create a fixture at tests/fixtures/fake_project/ with: a package.json (bun-plain stack), a state/fake_project/tasks.json with 1 pending task, a stub verify command that always passes. Register it in a test-only projects.yaml fixture. Spawn a dry-run cycle; assert: (a) worktree creates, (b) tasks.json is read correctly from project.path, (c) verification runs, (d) task-done detection reads from project.path. This is the regression guard so the state-path inconsistency from gs-166 cannot silently recur. Skip when running in CI without a usable bun binary.

Add test coverage for runBootstrap in src/bootstrap.ts covering the force path re-running on an existing scaffolded project WITHOUT a proposal dir — should re-create the proposal dir without re-scaffolding (since scaffold files already exist). Exercises the branch where dirExists=true, dirEmpty=false, proposalPath doesn't exist. Verifies result.createdScaffold === false even though force was true but nothing to force-overwrite.

Add a `generalstaff register <project-id>` CLI subcommand that reads state/<id>/tasks.json + a generated hands_off.yaml + project metadata and appends the project to projects.yaml. Prompts for confirmation (y/N) before editing; rejects if project id already registered. Error messages reference the bootstrap staging dir (`.generalstaff-proposal/`) if the project hasn't been bootstrapped yet. Tests covering: add new project, reject duplicate id, reject without confirmation, reject if state/<id>/ doesn't exist. Note: this modifies projects.yaml which is in hands_off — add an explicit exception for this command (it's the tool's own write path to its own config, equivalent to `init`). Document the exception in the command's help text. Depends on gs-166, gs-167.

Harden `src/reviewer.ts` JSON parser to tolerate unescaped inner content in `task_evidence[]` without false-negative rollback. Context: across 2026-04-17→18 (24h window) ten cycles rolled back with `reason: "reviewer response was not valid JSON"` — every sampled failure was a `verdict: "verified"` response where Qwen emitted `"task": "status": "done"` (unescaped inner colon). The verification gate's five decision-critical fields (`verdict`, `scope_drift_files`, `hands_off_violations`, `silent_failures`, and a usable `reason`) must be extracted strictly; the observational `task_evidence[]` and `notes` fields must be extracted permissively (drop malformed items, don't fail the whole response). Fail LOUD only if `verdict` itself cannot be recovered. Add tests with fixtures drawn from the archived failing responses under state/generalstaff/cycles/20260417011019_86ct/ and 20260418005225_tzrd/ — the parser must produce `verified` on these inputs. Do NOT weaken scope-drift / hands-off strictness. See DESIGN.md §v4 and research-notes.md §2026-04-18 for full rationale.

Add a belt-and-braces tightening to the reviewer prompt in `src/prompts/reviewer.ts` that discourages the model from embedding quotes/colons in string field values. Ask for `task_evidence[].task` as a bare identifier (`"task": "gs-170"`) rather than a natural-language status summary. This complements gs-171 (parser robustness) — the parser change is the load-bearing fix; this is a cheap prompt nudge to reduce the rate at which malformed responses arrive in the first place. Add a regression test that the updated prompt still elicits the full verdict schema from at least Qwen3-Coder-Plus and (if Ollama is running) llama3.1:8b. Depends on gs-171.

Register `gamr` as the first non-dogfood managed project. Originally specced to use the new `generalstaff register` CLI (gs-170); during interactive execution 2026-04-18 we discovered gs-170's CLI reads `state/<id>/tasks.json` from GeneralStaff's root rather than the project repo (gs-166/gs-167's new state-path scheme). Bypassed the broken CLI and appended gamr to projects.yaml directly. State now lives at `gamr/state/gamr/{tasks.json,MISSION.md}` per the new scheme. Three Phase 3 generality gaps surfaced in this work: (a) register CLI state-path drift → gs-175 queued, (b) bootstrap engineer_command.sh scaffold has no prompt → gs-176 queued, (c) bootstrap engineer_command.sh has no worktree management → folded into gs-176. gamr's engineer_command.sh manually patched to mirror GS's run_bot.sh structure.

Run the first bot cycle against `gamr` after gs-173 registers it. One-shot `generalstaff cycle gamr` (or natural pick via dispatcher if gamr is the staler project at the time). Target: produce any valid greenfield task completion against the gamr scaffold, exercise the full engineer → verification → reviewer → commit-merge pipeline end-to-end on a non-dogfood codebase. Expected to surface further generality gaps — document each in research-notes.md under the gamr registration entry and queue follow-up tasks (do NOT patch inline; Phase 3's whole point is cataloguing these before batch-fixing). Success criterion for Phase 3 closure: three consecutive gamr cycles land `verified` without dispatcher-level changes between them. Depends on gs-173. CLOSED 2026-04-18T11:35 UTC: 3/3 verified cycles (gamr-001 sanity, gamr-002 layout, gamr-003 page), zero rollbacks, OpenRouter reviewer, average wall ~96s. Closure operationally fragile to manual-merge-between-cycles workaround until gs-177/gs-178 land. Full write-up: PHASE-3-COMPLETE-2026-04-18.md.

Fix `src/register.ts` state-path drift: line 48-50 currently reads `${getRootDir()}/state/${projectId}/tasks.json` (GeneralStaff root), inconsistent with `src/work_detection.ts` post-gs-166 which reads `${project.path}/state/${projectId}/tasks.json` (target repo). For dogfood the two paths coincide and the bug is invisible; for any non-dogfood project they diverge and the register CLI cannot find tasks.json even when it exists in the correct (new) place. Surfaced 2026-04-18 during interactive execution of gs-173 — had to bypass the CLI and edit projects.yaml manually. Fix: change register.ts to use `${resolve(opts.projectPath)}/state/${projectId}/tasks.json`. Add a test using a non-dogfood projectPath fixture so this drift can't reappear. Update register.test.ts for the new contract.

Fix `src/bootstrap.ts` engineer_command.sh template: the bootstrap-generated script for new (non-dogfood) projects is just `claude -p --dangerously-skip-permissions` with NO PROMPT and NO WORKTREE MANAGEMENT — when invoked by the dispatcher, claude has no instructions and the bot would write to the main working tree (clobbering Ray's interactive work). Surfaced 2026-04-18 during gs-173: had to manually patch gamr/engineer_command.sh to (1) create/manage `.bot-worktree` on `bot/work` branch, (2) bun install in worktree, (3) invoke `claude -p` with a full prompt scoped to the project's tasks.json + hands_off + verify command + budget. Use the manually-patched gamr/engineer_command.sh as the template — parameterize project_id, hands-off path, verify command. Add a test using a temp-dir bootstrap that verifies the generated script contains both the worktree-add line and a non-empty prompt arg to claude. Without this, every newly-bootstrapped project's first bot cycle fails immediately.

Implement dispatcher behavior for auto_merge=false / chained-cycles tension per DESIGN.md §v5(a). CLOSED 2026-04-18: extracted decideBotBranchHandling helper (pure 4-way truth table over auto_merge × branch-exists × unmerged>0), wired into src/cycle.ts step 2 (was lines 461-507). When auto_merge=false AND branch has unmerged commits, dispatcher now ACCUMULATES on bot/work instead of aborting — verified-cycle commits pile across the session, master untouched until human merge. Cycle N+1's worktree starts from accumulated head, so tasks.json updates from cycle N propagate naturally. 6 new helper tests in tests/cycle.test.ts. 984/984 tests pass, tsc clean. Eliminates the one-cycle-per-session ceiling that was forcing manual-merge-between-cycles in this morning's gamr Phase 3 closure. DESIGN.md §v5 §Definition-of-done satisfied modulo the integration-test for the successor-sees-predecessor semantic, which is exercised by tests/helpers/verify_full_non_dogfood_cycle.ts indirectly + the existing cycle integration tests; a dedicated multi-cycle integration test could be added as gs-180 if observed flake warrants it.

Fix audit-log writes dirtying the GS working tree mid-session. CLOSED 2026-04-18 with option (b) — narrow whitelist for state/<id>/PROGRESS.jsonl in isWorkingTreeClean via two new exported helpers (isExemptFromCleanTreeCheck, filterCleanTreePorcelain). 11 new tests cover the exemption pattern (positive + negative cases) and the porcelain-filtering logic, including the verbatim real-world fixture from cycle 20260418112438_fcsb. Existing safety surface preserved for tasks.json, MISSION.md, session.pid, cycle artifacts. Original task body: Fix audit-log writes dirtying the GS working tree mid-session. Surfaced 2026-04-18 during gs-174: when the bot ran a gamr cycle, audit.ts appended events to state/gamr/PROGRESS.jsonl (which lives in GS's tracked working tree per audit.ts:21). The next cycle's clean-tree safety check then refused to run a generalstaff cycle, structurally blocking cross-project chaining within a session.

Add `generalstaff doctor` CLI subcommand that fails fast with a clear human-readable message when the runtime environment isn't ready for a session. Checks: (1) `bun --version` resolves and is >=1.3.0; (2) `claude --version` resolves; (3) `git --version` resolves; (4) the project root has projects.yaml that parses; (5) for each registered project, project.path exists and is a git repo; (6) for each registered project with concurrency_detection=worktree, no `.bot-worktree` exists (or if it does, fail with the path so the user can investigate). Output: one-line PASS or itemized FAIL with remediation hints (e.g. "bun not in PATH — install from https://bun.sh"). Touch points: src/cli.ts (new subcommand) + new src/doctor.ts module + tests/doctor.test.ts. Pure read-only — no side effects, no file writes. Useful for new users hitting first-launch friction. Should pass under `bun test && bun x tsc --noEmit`.

Add `--summary` flag to `generalstaff status` that prints today's session metrics: total cycles, verified count, verification-failed count, average cycle duration, total bot wall-clock minutes, last session end timestamp. Source: read state/_fleet/PROGRESS.jsonl + each project's PROGRESS.jsonl, filter to events from today (UTC midnight cutoff), aggregate. Format as one-line-per-metric for terminal readability; `--json` flag (already exists for status) should also work with --summary. Touch points: src/cli.ts + maybe a small helper in src/audit.ts or src/digest.ts. Tests covering: a synthetic PROGRESS.jsonl fixture with cross-day events filtered correctly, empty fixture returning zeros, --json output structure. Should pass under `bun test && bun x tsc --noEmit`.

Improve src/projects.ts parse error messages to cite line numbers and suggest fixes. Today's behavior: a malformed projects.yaml gives a generic YAML parse error pointing at the wrong line (the `yaml` library's column-counting is off-by-one in some cases per its own docs). Goal: when parsing fails, walk the parsed AST (or fall back to the raw error from `yaml`) and produce a message of the form: `projects.yaml line X: <specific issue>. Likely cause: <hint>. Fix: <suggested edit>.` Common cases to handle: missing required field (id, path, hands_off), hands_off list empty (Hard Rule #5 violation), hands_off path entry that doesn't look like a glob, project id with invalid chars, duplicate project id. At least 5 fixture-based tests covering these cases. Should pass under `bun test && bun x tsc --noEmit`.

[INTERACTIVE-ONLY — touches scripts/run_bot.sh which is hands-off; bot will roll back if it tries] Add a within-project task-picker tiebreak rule to scripts/run_bot.sh's claude-p prompt. Today: the prompt says "pick the highest-priority unfinished task" with no defined tiebreak between same-priority entries, and the LLM picks unpredictably (potentially the last task in the JSON, the most recently mentioned, or random). On 2026-04-18 morning we worked around this by demoting gs-173 from P1 to P2 so gs-171 was uniquely P1. Structural fix: add one prompt line — `If multiple tasks have the same priority, pick the lowest gs-NNN (numeric suffix) first.` — to scripts/run_bot.sh AND to the bootstrap-template prompt in src/bootstrap.ts (so newly-bootstrapped projects inherit it). Test: regression for the bootstrap template's emitted prompt containing this line. Workaround-fine for now — only worth doing when same-priority collisions become routine.

Extend src/bootstrap.ts:detectStack to recognize Python projects (sniff for pyproject.toml, requirements.txt, setup.py, setup.cfg, .python-version) AND handle the chicken-and-egg where stack-signal files don't exist at register-time. Solutions: accept --stack=<python|node|bun|...> arg to bootstrap/register, OR sniff existing engineer_command.sh content for uv/python/pip patterns. Surfaced 2026-04-18 raybrain bootstrap: gs-176 template generated Bun-first commands for a Python project, other Claude session manually patched. Bot-doable.

Make src/session.ts hot-reload projects.yaml between cycles. Surfaced 2026-04-18 raybrain registration: session 7 launched with only generalstaff+gamr; raybrain registered ~30 min into session 7; override file pointed at raybrain but picker fell through because raybrain wasn't in cached projects list. Fix: re-load loadProjects() between cycles. Tests: mutate fixture projects.yaml mid-loop and assert next pick sees the new project. Bot-doable.

Add fast-fail backoff to dispatcher: if N consecutive cycles on same project end with engineer_exit_code != 0 in less than M seconds wall, soft-skip that project for rest of session and log a warning. Surfaced 2026-04-18 raybrain shakedown: broken engineer_command.sh exited code 2 in 1 second per cycle, dispatcher spun retrying at ~1/sec producing 82 failed cycles in 2 minutes before STOP fired. Defaults: N=3, M=60s. Add ProgressEventType project_soft_skipped. Bot-doable.
